meta.name: NCS-6 Need for Cognition Scale Brief
meta.fullName: Need for Cognition Scale - 6 Item Short Form
meta.description: The NCS-6 is a brief version of the Need for Cognition Scale, measuring an individual's tendency to engage in and enjoy effortful thinking. Higher scores indicate a greater intrinsic motivation to think deeply about complex topics.
meta.author: Cacioppo JT, Petty RE (1984)
meta.reference: Cacioppo JT, Petty RE, Kao CF. The efficient assessment of need for cognition. J Pers Assess. 1984;48(3):306-307.
meta.instruction: For each of the statements below, please indicate how much you agree that the statement is characteristic of you.

questions.text: I would prefer complex to simple problems
I like to have the responsibility of handling a situation that requires a lot of thinking
Thinking is not my idea of fun
I would rather do something that requires little thought than something that is sure to challenge my thinking abilities
I really enjoy a task that involves coming up with new solutions to problems
I prefer my life to be filled with puzzles that I must solve

ranges.label: Low Need for Cognition
ranges.description: You tend to prefer tasks that require less cognitive effort. You may find complex thinking somewhat unpleasant or unengaging.
suggestions: Try small, engaging puzzles or brainteasers to build cognitive enjoyment
Break complex tasks into smaller, manageable steps
Explore topics that genuinely interest you to make thinking more rewarding
Consider that cognitive engagement can be developed with practice

ranges.label: Moderate Need for Cognition
ranges.description: You have an average tendency toward cognitive engagement. You sometimes enjoy thinking deeply but may prefer simpler tasks at other times.
suggestions: Challenge yourself with new learning opportunities
Engage in discussions that require critical thinking
Read diverse materials to expand intellectual curiosity

ranges.label: Above Average Need for Cognition
ranges.description: You generally enjoy thinking deeply and find intellectual challenges satisfying. You tend to seek out cognitively demanding activities.
suggestions: Share your love of thinking with others through teaching or mentoring
Balance cognitive engagement with rest and relaxation

ranges.label: High Need for Cognition
ranges.description: You strongly enjoy effortful thinking and are highly motivated to engage in complex cognitive activities. You find great satisfaction in intellectual challenges.
suggestions: Continue embracing your love of learning and thinking
Seek out challenging problems that stretch your abilities
Remember to be patient with those who may prefer less cognitive effort
